sections.title: Intake
missing: treasury_amount_lovelace is missing for a treasury withdrawal
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Intersect Technical Steering Committee support activities for 12 months, including community engagement, protocol governance, and independent technical review.
Recipient: Intersect 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v
Deliverables: WP 1: Community-Facing Technical Coordination for 256,000 ADA, WP 2: Protocol Governance and Evolution for 832,000 ADA, WP 3: Technical Review for 105,000 ADA
Deadline/expiry: 12 months; pilot independent technical review programme launching in 2027
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Intersect requests $298,250, equivalent to 1,193,000 ADA, to support TSC activities for 12 months.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The budget is allocated as 256,000 ADA for WP1, 832,000 ADA for WP2, and 105,000 ADA for WP3.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): WP2 funds the Parameter Committee, CIP editors, and Hard Fork Working Group.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): WP3 funds a pilot independent technical review programme launching in 2027 with conflict-of-interest provisions, published recusals, and open reviewer selection against documented criteria.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): All expenditure will be fully accounted for and any unspent funds returned to the Cardano treasury.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Execution of the work packages is contingent on approval of the IntersectMBO budget proposal.
missing: Independent evidence for: WP3 funds a pilot independent technical review programme launching in 2027 with conflict-of-interest provisions, published recusals, and open reviewer selection against documented criteria.
Independent evidence for: All expenditure will be fully accounted for and any unspent funds returned to the Cardano treasury.

Independent evidence for: Independent audits and assurance for the proposal will be provided by Appold, with possible reviews including deliverable verification, control testing, spend validation, and milestone evidence assessment.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Intersect requests $298,250, equivalent to 1,193,000 ADA, to support TSC activities for 12 months." though 1 review blocker(s) remain open.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "WP3 funds a pilot independent technical review programme launching in 2027 with conflict-of-interest provisions, published recusals, and open reviewer selection against documented criteria." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: evidence is too thin for a directional vote while 1 blocker(s) remain open.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
